gallery_desc: Three roadmaps from the same inflection point. Convergence is prerequisite to both futures.

roadmaps.desc: Refactor to layered architecture: pure core, IO runtime, thin CLI

estimates.risk: low — mechanical refactoring, all tests gate every node

roadmaps.desc: Extract the algebra as a stable protocol — wire format, properties, cross-repo, economics

estimates.risk: medium — plan nodes need decomposition, cross-repo is uncharted

roadmaps.desc: The DAG is the program — dissolve spec/code/test/governance into one artifact

estimates.risk: high — graph-rewriting is a plan node, self-reference is philosophically deep

topology.note: Both futures fork from convergence. They are independent and can be pursued in parallel or sequentially. They reconverge if the protocol standard's compilation target meets programs-as-graphs' inversion of control.
